title: Pentameris tortuosa
intro: Pentameris tortuosa, also known as Twisted Hair Grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Pentameris tortuosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pentameris tortuosa is classified within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The genus Pentameris is part of the large family Poaceae, which includes many species of grasses. This classification places Pentameris tortuosa within a group of cool-season grasses. Pentameris tortuosa is also known to be closely related to the genus Festuca.
